message.content:
1) The portrait on the right occupies roughly **60-70% of the viewport height** (dominant vertical presence).  
2) No, the person’s full body is **not visible**—the image is cropped at the waist/hips, so the feet are cut off, and they are not standing on the baseline.  
3) Yes, the portrait looks **large and dominant** (visually prominent against the background, balancing the text on the left).  
4) Yes, there are **cropping/cut-off issues**: the lower body (waist down) is truncated, and the top of the head is slightly close to the frame but not severely cropped.  
5) The composition feels **balanced** overall: the large portrait (right) and bold text (left) create visual harmony, with the dynamic background unifying both elements. The dominance of the portrait complements the text without overwhelming it.
